slug: 你手机电池里装的是什么_锂为什么会爆炸
title: 你手机电池里装的是什么——锂为什么会爆炸

outline.concept: Lithium is the metal most eager to give up its electron — that's why it stores so much energy, and also why it's dangerous. The liquid electrolyte inside your battery isn't water; it's a flammable organic solvent, because lithium reacts violently with water and would literally ignite on contact.
outline.query: 电池 漏液 液体 腐蚀 气味 烧焦

outline.concept: Inside the battery, a polymer separator only ~20 micrometers thick (thinner than a hair) keeps the anode and cathode apart while letting lithium ions pass through. If this separator is punctured, the two electrodes touch directly — an internal short circuit — and all that stored energy dumps as heat in an instant.
outline.query: 手机 摔 背面 撞击 弯曲 座椅压到

outline.concept: When charging too fast or over many cycles, lithium doesn't deposit smoothly on the anode — it grows needle-like metallic structures called dendrites. These dendrites grow toward the cathode and can eventually pierce the separator, causing that internal short circuit from the inside out.
outline.query: 快充 充电一夜 掉电快 电池健康度 循环次数

outline.concept: Thermal runaway: once a short circuit generates heat, the flammable electrolyte decomposes and releases oxygen. That oxygen feeds more combustion → more heat → more decomposition. This self-accelerating loop goes from warm to explosion in seconds, and nothing inside the battery can stop it once it starts.
outline.query: 手机 发烫 烫手 充电发热 电池鼓包 自动关机
